pattern: Maximizing the smallest same-group gap is a bipartite threshold question: the answer is the largest distance below which the too-close pairs can still be two-colored apart.
hints: If you fix a target t, every pair closer than t must land in opposite groups, which is exactly a graph 2-coloring constraint.
Those forced-apart pairs can be satisfied if and only if the graph of sub-t edges is bipartite, and feasibility only worsens as t grows.
Binary search the sorted distinct distances for the largest t whose sub-t graph stays bipartite; with only two points the factor is defined as zero.

edge_inputs.description: odd cycle of near points is not bipartite

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 12))
    pts = [[rng.randrange(-20, 21), rng.randrange(-20, 21)] for _ in range(n)]
    return [pts]
stress.note: small n so the brute oracle over colorings stays fast

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 14))
    pts = [[rng.randrange(-1000, 1001), rng.randrange(-1000, 1001)] for _ in range(n)]
    return [pts]
